category_title: High-attaining students without additional needs who perceive a lack of teacher support in inclusive settings
minimal_context: The student is preparing to perform a scene from A Midsummer Night's Dream in front of their peers to practice reading lines with confidence and using body language.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to practice performing your scene from A Midsummer Night's Dream in front of the class.

STUDENT: I really don't want to do this. I have a strong dislike of drama.

TUTOR: The goal is to read your lines with confidence and use body language and gestures.

STUDENT: I know the lines, but I don't want to stand up and do the performance part.

TUTOR: Everyone needs to practice performing in front of people today.

STUDENT: Some people judge you... I just don’t like performing in front of people.

options.A: Read and discuss the unfolding plot elements of the scene with the student during the activity.
options.B: Give the student a warning about their refusal to perform, but do not discipline them further.
options.C: Provide the student with freedom and autonomy in how they approach the performance activity.
options.D: Use stronger gravity moves to demand immediate focus (e.g., "Focus on this! If not, I'll cancel the project.").
